subject: [PATCH v3 0/2] x86/tdx: Port I/O emulation fixes

reviews.author: Edgecombe, P
reviews.summary: reviewer noted that the zero-extension for 32-bit port I/O operations should be clarified in the GHCI spec, and suggested merging the patch despite some potential complications
reviews.raw_body:
We are working on getting the GHCI spec amended to clarify who is supposed to do
this zero-extending and masking, host or guest. For this and the similar
tdvmcalls. The process involves getting all VMMs in agreement.

Today I think the spec doesn't say to *not* do it, so I think it is reasonable
to merge this, but there is some small risk of complications depending on how
that discussion goes.

reviews.author: Dave Hansen
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Dave Hansen requested clarification on why the 32-bit port I/O case needs to clear RAX[63:32], and suggested expanding a comment in patch 2 with a table describing which RAX bits each IN form writes vs preserves.

Reviewer Dave Hansen suggested replacing the GENMASK() macro usage with a switch statement to handle the three possible cases of port I/O operations (1, 2, and 4 bytes), which would simplify the code and eliminate the need for special cases.

2026-05-27.patch_summary: This patch series addresses two technical inaccuracies in the TDX guest port I/O emulation code, fixing an off-by-one error and ensuring correct zero-extension for 32-bit port I/O operations. The first patch corrects a calculation error in the GENMASK macro usage, while the second patch ensures that the result of 32-bit port I/O operations is correctly extended to the full 64-bit RAX register as required by the x86 architecture.

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about the verbosity of the GENMASK_ULL() macro usage and proposes an alternative implementation inspired by kvm's assign_register(). They plan to restructure the code in v2.

reviews.author: Dave Hansen
reviews.summary: Reviewer Dave Hansen expressed concern that the code uses inline assembly to implement port I/O operations, which he finds unintelligible and overly clever. He suggested using MOV[BWL] instructions instead, as they have the same semantics as IN[BWL] and can be used to match the behavior of INL, which zeros the upper 64 bits.

reviews.author: Dave Hansen
reviews.summary: Reviewer Dave Hansen suggested consolidating the port I/O handling code with kvm's assign_register() function, citing excessive copy-pasting as a reason.
reviews.raw_body:
I think I could stand this if it consolidated this site with kvm's
assign_register(). The copy/paste is too much to bear.

reviews.author: David Laight
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where the IN instruction writes to RAX[63:32] bits, and requested clarification on why this is handled differently than other instructions.

Reviewer requested that the code be rewritten as normal arithmetic code to improve readability

2026-05-28.patch_summary: This patch series addresses two technical inaccuracies in the TDX guest port I/O emulation code, fixing an off-by-one error and ensuring correct zero-extension for 32-bit port I/O operations. The first patch corrects a calculation error in the GENMASK macro usage, while the second patch ensures that the result of 32-bit port I/O operations is correctly extended to the full 64-bit RAX register as required by the x86 architecture.
